deck.title: Word Knowledge — Prefixes & Suffixes
deck.description: Common ASVAB prefixes and suffixes. Decode unfamiliar words by their parts.

cards.explanation: Examples: unhappy (not happy), undo (reverse an action), unable (not able).
Examples: redo (do again), return (go back), rebuild (build again).
Examples: hopeless (without hope), fearless (without fear), endless (without end).
Examples: preview (view before), prepare (ready before), predict (say before).
Examples: postwar (after war), postpone (put after), postscript (added after writing).
Examples: misspell (spell wrongly), misjudge (judge wrongly), misplace (put in wrong place).
Examples: readable (able to be read), edible (able to be eaten), flexible (able to bend).
Examples: kindness (state of being kind), darkness (state of being dark), happiness (state of being happy).
Examples: agreement (act of agreeing), movement (act of moving), achievement (result of achieving).
Examples: antebellum (before war), antecedent (something that comes before), anterior (front-positioned).
Examples: antiwar (against war), antibiotic (against bacteria), antifreeze (prevents freezing).
Examples: circumnavigate (sail around), circumference (distance around a circle), circumvent (go around).
Examples: international (between nations), intervene (come between), intersect (cross between).
Examples: clarity (quality of being clear), purity (state of being pure), activity (state of being active).

cards.back: full of, having the quality of (suffix)
cards.explanation: Examples: dangerous (full of danger), joyous (full of joy), nervous (full of nerves).
Examples: contradict (speak against), contraband (goods against the law), contrary (opposite in nature).
Examples: intramural (within walls/within a school), intravenous (within a vein), intrastate (within a state).
Examples: omnipotent (all-powerful), omnivore (eats all kinds of food), omnipresent (present everywhere).
Examples: solitude (state of being alone), gratitude (quality of being thankful), magnitude (quality of greatness).

cards.back: in the manner or direction of (suffix)
cards.explanation: Examples: clockwise (in the direction of a clock), lengthwise (in the direction of length), otherwise (in another manner).
